name: Wyeth v. Levine
question: Does federal law preempt state law in a personal injury action against a drug manufacturer for failing to include an appropriate warning label where the drug in question met the labeling requirements of the Food and Drug Administration?
description: A case in which the Court held that federal law did not preempt state law in a personal injury action against a drug manufacturer for failing to include an appropriate warning label, even where the drug in question met the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) labeling requirements.
